story.iWant: to resolve a workflow phase or agent role into a fully configured, instrumented, and optionally secured IAgentProvider
story.soThat: the engine can request providers by phase name without knowing about provider chains, health tracking, prompt registries, or security wrapping

tasks.task: - [ ] Create `packages/providers/src/role-based-agent-resolver.ts` - [ ] Define `RoleBasedAgentResolverOptions` interface with config, factory, health, promptRegistry, diagnostics, costTracker?, sanitizer?, logger? - [ ] Implement constructor that accepts single options object (no positional params) - [ ] Store all dependencies as private readonly fields - [ ] Initialize `chains` as `Map<AgentType, IProviderChain>` - [ ] Implement `getAgentForPhase(phase, context)` that maps phase to role via `config.phaseRoleMap` with `DEFAULT_PHASE_ROLE_MAP` fallback, then delegates to `getAgentForRole()` - [ ] Write unit tests for phase-to-role mapping and custom phaseRoleMap overrides

task.title: Implement getAgentForRole with chain creation and security wrapping

tasks.task: - [ ] Implement `getAgentForRole(role, context)` that calls `getOrCreateChain(role)` then `chain.getProvider()` - [ ] Pass `{ agentType: role, ...context }` to `chain.getProvider()` - [ ] Wrap returned provider with `SecureAgentProvider` if `this.sanitizer` is provided - [ ] Return unwrapped provider when no sanitizer is configured - [ ] Write unit tests: provider is instrumented, provider is secure when sanitizer exists, provider is unwrapped when no sanitizer

- [ ] Implement `getTaskConfig(role, taskOverrides?)` with merge precedence: defaults < role config < task overrides - [ ] Build base from `this.config.defaults` (allowedTools, maxBudgetUsd, permissionMode) - [ ] Build roleLevel from `this.config.roles?.[role]` only for defined fields (use conditional spread) - [ ] Return merged `{ ...base, ...roleLevel, ...taskOverrides }` - [ ] Write unit tests: merge precedence, undefined roleConfig falls back to defaults, task overrides win

task.title: Implement getPrompt delegation and getOrCreateChain with empty-array fallback

tasks.task: - [ ] Implement `getPrompt(role, providerName, vars)` delegating to `this.promptRegistry.render(role, providerName, vars)` - [ ] Implement private `getOrCreateChain(role)` with lazy ProviderChain creation and Map caching - [ ] Handle empty-array fallback: `providerChain: []` must fall back to defaults (check `.length`, not just `??`) - [ ] Pass ProviderChainOptions object `{ entries, factory, health, diagnostics, costTracker, logger }` to ProviderChain constructor - [ ] Write unit tests: getPrompt delegates correctly, empty providerChain falls back, chains are cached

task.title: Export all new modules from providers index

tasks.task: - [ ] Add `export { RoleBasedAgentResolver } from './role-based-agent-resolver.js'` to `packages/providers/src/index.ts` - [ ] Add `export type { RoleBasedAgentResolverOptions } from './role-based-agent-resolver.js'` - [ ] Verify all Epic 9 Story 3-8 modules are exported: errors, provider-health, agent-provider-factory, provider-chain, agent-prompt-registry, secure-agent-provider, instrumented-agent-provider, role-based-agent-resolver - [ ] Run `pnpm --filter @tamma/providers run typecheck` to verify compilation

acceptanceCriteria.criterion: `getAgentForPhase('PLAN_GENERATION', ctx)` resolves to the 'architect' role via DEFAULT_PHASE_ROLE_MAP and returns an IAgentProvider
Custom `phaseRoleMap` in config overrides DEFAULT_PHASE_ROLE_MAP (e.g., mapping PLAN_GENERATION to 'researcher')
`getAgentForRole('implementer', ctx)` returns an InstrumentedAgentProvider from the role's provider chain
When `sanitizer` is provided, returned provider is wrapped with SecureAgentProvider
When `sanitizer` is not provided, returned provider is NOT wrapped (returned directly from chain)
`getTaskConfig('implementer', overrides)` merges: defaults < role config < task overrides (last wins)
`getTaskConfig('researcher')` with no role config returns defaults only
Role with `providerChain: []` falls back to `config.defaults.providerChain` (empty array is NOT treated as configured)
`getPrompt(role, provider, vars)` delegates to `IAgentPromptRegistry.render()` with sanitized vars and returns rendered template
Constructor accepts `RoleBasedAgentResolverOptions` object (no positional parameters)
Uses `DiagnosticsQueue` from `@tamma/shared/src/telemetry/`, NOT `ToolHookRegistry` from mcp-client
All new exports are available from `packages/providers/src/index.ts`
`pnpm --filter @tamma/providers run typecheck` passes under strict TypeScript
`pnpm vitest run packages/providers/src/role-based-agent-resolver` passes with all unit tests green
Constructor throws when `defaults.providerChain` is empty or missing
`getOrCreateChain()` and `getTaskConfig()` reject forbidden keys (`__proto__`, `constructor`, `prototype`)
`getTaskConfig()` clamping: `maxBudgetUsd` override cannot exceed resolved ceiling, `bypassPermissions` requires env var, `allowedTools` is intersected
`getPrompt()` sanitizes `{{` and `}}` from variable values to prevent template injection
`getAgentForRole()` logs warn when sanitizer is not configured
`dispose()` clears the chains Map
`getRoleForPhase(phase)` returns the role synchronously without creating a provider
Class implements `IRoleBasedAgentResolver` interface

docs.doc: Story specification with full class implementation and code snippets
Configuration types: AgentsConfig, WorkflowPhase, DEFAULT_PHASE_ROLE_MAP, ProviderChainEntry, AgentRoleConfig
ProviderChain class with fallback, health check, budget check, and instrumentation wrapping
AgentProviderFactory that creates IAgentProvider by name string with wrapAsAgent()
ProviderHealthTracker circuit breaker with sliding window failures and half-open probing
AgentPromptRegistry with 6-level resolution chain and {{variable}} template rendering
DiagnosticsQueue and InstrumentedAgentProvider for usage tracking
Engine integration showing how RoleBasedAgentResolver is consumed
Technical architecture (naming conventions, error handling, event sourcing)
Project coding standards, TypeScript strict mode, naming conventions

code.file: RoleBasedAgentResolver class with options-object constructor, getAgentForPhase(), getAgentForRole(), getTaskConfig(), getPrompt(), getOrCreateChain()
Unit tests covering all acceptance criteria
Export RoleBasedAgentResolver, RoleBasedAgentResolverOptions, and all Epic 9 modules

dependencies.dependency: ICostTracker interface (optional dependency for budget checking)

constraints.constraint: Requires Story 9-1 (config types), Story 9-5 (ProviderChain), Story 9-6 (AgentPromptRegistry), Story 9-7 (SecureAgentProvider) to be completed first
All code must compile under TypeScript strict mode (strict: true, noImplicitAny, noImplicitReturns)
Files use kebab-case; interfaces use I-prefix; types use PascalCase; constants use SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE
Use .js extensions in import paths for ESM compatibility
Use options-object pattern (RoleBasedAgentResolverOptions), NOT positional parameters
getTaskConfig() must not mutate config objects -- always create new objects via spread
Use DiagnosticsQueue from @tamma/shared/src/telemetry/, NOT ToolHookRegistry from mcp-client
No API keys or secrets in test fixtures; SecureAgentProvider wrapping is opt-in via sanitizer presence
providerChain: [] must be detected and fall back to defaults -- ?? does NOT trigger for empty arrays
Providers are NOT pooled -- each getAgentForRole() call may create a fresh provider via the factory chain

interface.name: IAgentProvider
interface.description: Agent provider interface with executeTask(config, onProgress?), isAvailable(), dispose()

interface.name: AgentTaskConfig
interface.description: Task configuration with prompt, cwd, model?, maxBudgetUsd?, allowedTools?, permissionMode?, outputFormat?, sessionId?

interface.name: AgentsConfig
interface.description: Multi-agent configuration with defaults (AgentRoleConfig), roles? (Partial<Record<AgentType, Partial<AgentRoleConfig>>>), phaseRoleMap? (Partial<Record<WorkflowPhase, AgentType>>)

interface.name: ProviderChainEntry
interface.description: Provider chain entry with provider (string), model?, apiKey?, config?

interface.name: AgentRoleConfig
interface.description: Role configuration with providerChain, allowedTools?, maxBudgetUsd?, permissionMode?, systemPrompt?, providerPrompts?

interface.name: IProviderChain
interface.description: Ordered provider chain interface with getProvider(context) that returns InstrumentedAgentProvider, handles health checks, budget checks, and fallback

interface.name: IAgentProviderFactory
interface.description: Factory interface that creates IAgentProvider by name string; LLM providers eagerly wrapped via wrapAsAgent()

interface.name: IProviderHealthTracker
interface.description: Circuit breaker interface per provider+model key with isHealthy(), recordFailure(), recordSuccess()

interface.name: IAgentPromptRegistry
interface.description: Prompt template resolver interface with 6-level fallback chain and {{variable}} rendering via render(role, providerName, vars)

interface.name: SecureAgentProvider
interface.description: Decorator wrapping IAgentProvider with input/output content sanitization

interface.name: IContentSanitizer
interface.description: Interface with sanitize(input) and sanitizeOutput(output) for HTML stripping, zero-width char removal, prompt injection detection

interface.name: DiagnosticsQueue
interface.description: Event queue with synchronous emit() and background drain to processor

interface.name: ICostTracker
interface.description: Cost tracking interface with checkLimit(), recordUsage(), generateReport()

interface.name: IRoleBasedAgentResolver
interface.description: Public interface for the resolver: getAgentForPhase(), getAgentForRole(), getTaskConfig(), getPrompt(), getRoleForPhase(), dispose()

interface.name: ILogger
interface.description: Logger interface with debug(), info(), warn(), error() methods

interface.name: DEFAULT_PHASE_ROLE_MAP
interface.description: Constant mapping all 8 WorkflowPhase values to AgentType defaults (e.g., PLAN_GENERATION -> architect)

standards.standard: 80% line coverage, 75% branch coverage, 85% function coverage
TDD: write tests before or alongside implementation
Mock all external dependencies; no real API calls in unit tests

ideas.idea: Test: getAgentForPhase('PLAN_GENERATION') maps to 'architect' role via DEFAULT_PHASE_ROLE_MAP
Test: getAgentForPhase('CODE_GENERATION') maps to 'implementer' role
Test: custom phaseRoleMap overrides DEFAULT_PHASE_ROLE_MAP (e.g., PLAN_GENERATION -> 'researcher')
Test: getAgentForRole creates ProviderChain from role-specific providerChain entries
Test: getAgentForRole uses defaults.providerChain when role has no providerChain configured
Test: getAgentForRole with providerChain: [] falls back to defaults (empty array guard)
Test: returned provider is wrapped with SecureAgentProvider when sanitizer is provided
Test: returned provider is NOT wrapped when sanitizer is absent
Test: getTaskConfig merge precedence -- task overrides > role config > defaults
Test: getTaskConfig with undefined roleConfig falls back to defaults only
Test: getTaskConfig with partial role config merges correctly (only defined fields override)
Test: getPrompt delegates to AgentPromptRegistry.render() with correct args
Test: getPrompt passes vars through to render()
Test: constructor accepts RoleBasedAgentResolverOptions (options object, not positional)
Test: ProviderChain instances are cached per role (Map reuse on second call)
Test: different roles create different ProviderChain instances
Test: fallback works when first provider in chain fails (delegates to ProviderChain behavior)
Test: constructor throws when defaults.providerChain is empty or missing
Test: getOrCreateChain rejects forbidden keys (__proto__, constructor, prototype)
Test: getTaskConfig rejects forbidden keys (__proto__, constructor, prototype)
Test: getAgentForPhase rejects forbidden role key from phaseRoleMap
Test: getTaskConfig clamping — maxBudgetUsd override cannot exceed resolved ceiling
Test: getTaskConfig clamping — bypassPermissions requires TAMMA_ALLOW_BYPASS_PERMISSIONS=true
Test: getTaskConfig clamping — allowedTools override is intersected with resolved tools
Test: getPrompt sanitizes {{ and }} from variable values
Test: getAgentForRole logs debug message with role and context
Test: getAgentForRole logs warn when sanitizer is not configured
Test: dispose() clears the chains Map
Test: getRoleForPhase returns role synchronously without creating provider
Test: class implements IRoleBasedAgentResolver interface
